commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram target prefix normalization to strip bare `group:`, `channel:`, and `user:` prefixes and adds regression tests for those cases.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current recovery replays `entry.to`, current Telegram normalization preserves bare `group:-...`, and `resolveChatId` rejects that value. I did not run a live Telegram retry in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The next action is contributor or maintainer proof, not a ClawSweeper code repair: add redacted real Telegram delivery evidence and decide whether the broader prefix stripping is acceptable.

target.title: fix(telegram): strip kind prefixes in retry payloads to prevent message loss
target.body:
Fixes #85640

## Problem

Telegram group message retries fail permanently because `stripTelegramInternalPrefixes` only strips `group:` when it follows a `telegram:` prefix. When retry payloads carry bare kind prefixes like `group:-1003786193717`, the prefix survives:

```
to: "group:-1003786193717"  â†’  normalizeTelegramChatId  â†’  undefined
â†’ resolveChatId throws "Telegram recipient must be a numeric chat ID"
â†’ 5 retries  â†’  message permanently lost in delivery-queue/failed/
```

## Root Cause

`extensions/telegram/src/targets.ts:20` â€” the `group:` strip was guarded by `strippedTelegramPrefix === true`, meaning it only triggered after first stripping `telegram:`. Bare `group:` (no preceding `telegram:`) went through unchanged.

## Fix

One-line logic change in `stripTelegramInternalPrefixes`:

```diff
- if (strippedTelegramPrefix && /^group:/i.test(trimmed)) {
-   return trimmed.replace(/^group:/i, "").trim();
+ if (/^(group|channel|user):/i.test(trimmed)) {
+   return trimmed.replace(/^(group|channel|user):/i, "").trim();
```

Removes the `strippedTelegramPrefix` guard so kind prefixes are stripped unconditionally. Also extends coverage to `channel:` and `user:` which could trip the same path.

## Test Results

```
âś“ extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts â€” 35 passed (33 original + 3 new regression)
```

### Regression Tests Added

1. `strips group prefix without telegram prefix` â†’ `"-100123"` (was previously expected NOT to strip)
2. `strips channel prefix without telegram prefix` â†’ `"-100456"` (new)
3. `strips user prefix without telegram prefix` â†’ `"123456789"` (new)

## Real Behavior Proof

**Before (main branch):**
```typescript
stripTelegramInternalPrefixes("group:-1003786193717")
// â†’ "group:-1003786193717"  // PREFIX SURVIVES
normalizeTelegramChatId("group:-1003786193717")
// â†’ undefined  // NOT A VALID CHAT ID
// â†’ Telegram API rejects â†’ 5 retries â†’ message lost
```

**After (this PR):**
```typescript
stripTelegramInternalPrefixes("group:-1003786193717")
// â†’ "-1003786193717"  // PREFIX STRIPPED
normalizeTelegramChatId("-1003786193717")
// â†’ "-1003786193717"  // VALID NUMERIC CHAT ID
// â†’ Telegram API accepts â†’ message delivered
```

This unblocks all queued retry items that have been sitting in `delivery-queue/failed/` with the `"Telegram recipient must be a numeric chat ID"` error.
